back_translation: Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem twenty-nine years. His mother’s name was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.
He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, but not with a whole heart.
When the kingdom was firmly under his control, he executed the officials who had murdered the king his father.

But he did not put their children to death; rather he acted according to what is written in the Law, in the Book of Moses, where the LORD commanded, “Fathers shall not be put to death for their children, and children shall not be put to death for their fathers; each shall die for his own sin.”

Amaziah gathered the men of Judah and arranged them by their families under commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds throughout Judah and Benjamin. He numbered them from twenty years old and upward and found 300,000 choice men able to go to war, holding spear and shield.
He also hired 100,000 mighty warriors from Israel for a hundred talents of silver.
But a man of God came to him and said, “O king, do not let the army of Israel go with you, for the LORD is not with Israel—all these Ephraimites.
Even if you go and fight bravely in battle, God will make you fall before the enemy, for God has power to help and power to make fall.”
Amaziah asked the man of God, “Then what shall I do about the hundred talents I have given to the army of Israel?” The man of God answered, “The LORD is able to give you much more than this.”
So Amaziah dismissed the troops that had come from Ephraim to go home. They were very angry with Judah and went home in fierce anger.
Amaziah strengthened himself and led his army to the Valley of Salt, where he struck down 10,000 men of Seir.
The men of Judah also captured 10,000 alive, brought them to the top of the cliff, and threw them down from the top of the cliff so that they were all dashed to pieces.
But the troops that Amaziah had sent back, not letting them go to battle with him, raided the cities of Judah from Samaria to Beth-horon, killed 3,000 people, and took much plunder.
After Amaziah returned from striking down the Edomites, he brought the gods of the men of Seir and set them up as his own gods, bowed down before them, and burned incense to them.
Therefore the wrath of the LORD blazed against Amaziah, and He sent a prophet to him who said, “Why have you sought the gods of a people who could not deliver their own people from your hand?”

While the prophet was still speaking, the king said to him, “Have we appointed you a counselor to the king? Stop! Do you want to be struck down?” So the prophet stopped, but said, “I know that God has determined to destroy you, because you have done this and have not listened to my counsel.”
Then Amaziah king of Judah took counsel and sent word to Jehoash son of Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying, “Come, let us meet face to face.”

But Jehoash king of Israel answered Amaziah king of Judah, “A thistle in Lebanon sent word to a cedar in Lebanon, saying, ‘Give your daughter to my son as a wife.’ But a wild beast in Lebanon passed by and trampled the thistle.
You say, ‘Behold, I have defeated Edom,’ and your heart is lifted up to boast. Now stay at home. Why stir up trouble so that you fall, you and Judah with you?”
But Amaziah would not listen, for this was from God, in order to deliver them into the hand of Jehoash, because they had sought the gods of Edom.
So Jehoash king of Israel came up, and he and Amaziah king of Judah faced each other at Beth-shemesh in Judah.
And Judah was defeated before Israel, and each fled to his own home.

Jehoash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, son of Joash, son of Jehoahaz, at Beth-shemesh, and brought him to Jerusalem. He tore down the wall of Jerusalem from the Ephraim Gate to the Corner Gate, a distance of four hundred cubits.
He took all the gold and silver, together with all the vessels found in the house of God that Obed-edom kept, and the treasures in the royal palace, and also took hostages, then returned to Samaria.
Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah lived fifteen years after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel.
As for the rest of the acts of Amaziah, from beginning to end, are they not written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel?
From the time that Amaziah turned away from following the LORD, a conspiracy was formed against him in Jerusalem, and he fled to Lachish. But they sent men after him to Lachish and killed him there.
They brought his body back on horses and buried him with his fathers in the city of Judah.
